title: Tone and Atmosphere
tags: [tone, style]

# Tone and Atmosphere

Tone is the emotional register a reader expects a scene to honor —
whether a moment of danger plays as dread, as dark comedy, or as
matter-of-fact procedure. Tone drift is more forgivable scene-to-scene
than voice drift, because real stories modulate mood, but the overall
register (how much horror, how much humor) should be set once and
respected everywhere.

## Establishing tone early

The opening passages carry disproportionate weight in setting reader
expectations. A story that opens wry and turns grim without warning
will read as a tonal error even if the grim material is well written on
its own.

## Tone versus content boundaries

Tone is a texture choice; content boundaries are a hard limit. Do not
confuse "this is a dark story" (tone) with "this story includes graphic
violence" (a content boundary the author sets explicitly).
